RFP should be issued in September / October. Next steps : BDR : Update SFDC with this information Pierre : Send an email to Lyvio and Cédric asking for a discovery meeting + Showpad access Conclusion : Coming RFP for Sourcing, CLM, and Tailspend.
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope includes ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake alongside iContract and iSource ‚Äî strong AI product footprint in an S2C Pharma deal. Three AI SKUs (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) signal Zycus has positioned the deal with AI as a meaningful part of the value proposition.
• Qualified stage with zero Avoma meetings recorded, no SF Description, BD_Engagement Unengaged, no LastActivityDate. Opella is the consumer healthcare spin-off of Sanofi. The complete absence of any engagement evidence (no Description, no Avoma, no LastActivity) combined with Unengaged BD suggests the deal may be very early-stage or dormant.
• Low confidence: rich AI product scope (3 AI SKUs) but no engagement evidence to confirm AI as the buying driver. Held at 5 ‚Äî would move UP to 7+ with a verified Avoma session capturing buyer AI dialogue; would drop to 3 if the dormancy persists. The 3-AI-SKU positioning by Zycus is the only AI signal.

metrics.narrative: The buyer's business problem is documented in Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c: Opella is operating on inherited legacy SAP and Coupa systems from Sanofi that may not align with their new independent structure, and they lack dedicated solutions for Sourcing and Tail Spend management. The pain is tied to post-carve-out transformation (Opella spun off from Sanofi in April 2025 per Compelling_Event__c). However, no quantified impact has been captured—no cost savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or error-rate metric. The qualitative pain is confirmed (legacy misalignment, lack of sourcing and tail spend tools), but the business case remains unquantified. Zero discovery calls have occurred to validate or deepen this understanding. Quantifying the pain with Cédric or a senior buyer is the immediate gap to close.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Cédric = Evaluator, not Champion role)
SF Tasks 2026-06-15: Pierre email, auto-reply, no follow-up post-return
champion.narrative: Champion is weakly identified. Salesforce X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c names Cédric Elmaleth (Senior Director of procurement, in charge of tools and processes) as the champion. However, Cédric's Contact Role is Evaluator, not Champion, and all engagement has been one-way from Zycus—Pierre reached out 2026-06-15, received an auto-reply (paternity leave until 2026-06-25), and no buyer follow-up has occurred since Cédric's return 6 days ago (2026-06-25 to 2026-07-01). There is no evidence of Cédric advocating for Zycus, providing internal access, or driving the agenda. The champion is developing at best, and currently passive. The senior influencer Lyvio FRIMAT (VP, Global Head of Procurement COE) has not been engaged and may be a stronger champion candidate given his strategy role. Champion-building with Cédric (re-engage post-paternity leave) and escalating to Lyvio are the immediate priorities.

sources: SF Why_not_Zycus__c (SAP and Coupa ecosystem, CLM vendor selected)
SF Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c (AI-driven suite, tail spend ANA, automation)
competition.narrative: Competition is confirmed but not active-vendor competition—it is inherited-stack inertia. Salesforce Competitors__c is null, but Why_not_Zycus__c states 'Existing ecosystem with SAP and Coupa may favor incumbents or integrated extensions. CLM vendor already selected, reducing iContract footprint. Competitive pressure from established sourcing/tail spend players.' The competitive context is the legacy SAP and Coupa environment Opella inherited from the relevant stakeholder or Coupa (or an integrated partner like Ariba for sourcing) position themselves as the low-risk extension of the existing stack, they will have an advantage. The CLM vendor selection also constrains the iContract opportunity. The wedge is Zycus AI-driven capabilities (ANA for tail spend, Merlin suite, sourcing automation) that legacy SAP/Coupa lack, positioned as the modern layer for an independent Opella. The win requires demonstrating that staying with the inherited stack perpetuates Sanofi-era constraints, while Zycus delivers transformation-grade AI and insights. This positioning has not yet been tested with the buyer (zero calls).

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c (inherited legacy SAP/Coupa, lack of sourcing/tail spend tools)
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is partially identified but not buyer-validated. Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c states Opella is operating on inherited legacy SAP and Coupa systems that may not align with their new independent structure post-carve-out, and they lack dedicated Sourcing and Tail Spend solutions. The Compelling_Event__c field notes the April 2025 disinvestment from Sanofi as the transformation trigger. The pain is tied to operational misalignment (legacy tools not fit for an independent entity) and capability gaps (no sourcing or tail spend management). However, this is Zycus-inferred pain, not buyer-articulated pain—zero discovery calls have occurred, so no buyer quote or confirmation exists. The pain owner is likely senior procurement leadership (Lyvio FRIMAT, VP of Procurement COE, or Marie-Annick LURO, Global Head Procurement Effectiveness), but neither has been engaged. The immediate move is to validate and quantify this pain in discovery with Cédric and Lyvio.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (CLM RFP mentioned, possible vendor already selected)
Zero discovery calls to surface paper mechanics
paper_process.narrative: Paper process is unknown. No Salesforce fields capture contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel). Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so there is no forcing function from an expiring contract. The Next Step mentions CLM (contract lifecycle management) is part of the RFP scope and a CLM vendor may already be selected, which could complicate contracting workflow if Zycus iContract overlaps with the chosen CLM tool. No SI or partner involvement is documented. The deal is too early (Qualified, pre-discovery) for paper-process visibility, but the lack of any contracting or implementation scoping discussion is a gap to address once the buyer re-engages. Discovery should surface whether Opella's procurement transformation includes a preferred contracting process or partner.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (5 contacts, none EB/Executive Sponsor)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. All five contact roles are Evaluator (Cédric Elmaleth, Marie-Annick LURO, Emeline TURMEAU) or Influencer (Lyvio FRIMAT, Claire Muret Grimbert). No C-level, CFO, or budget holder is mapped. Salesforce Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false, and X10a_Sponsor__c is null. For a 150k EUR deal at a newly independent company undergoing procurement transformation, the absence of an Economic Buyer is a gate risk—commercial and pricing conversations cannot progress without identifying who controls the budget. The evidence shows only mid-level procurement roles engaged so far. The senior-most contact is Lyvio FRIMAT (VP, Global Head of Procurement Centre of Excellence), but he is tagged Influencer, not Economic Buyer, and has not been engaged (no calls, no tasks). Mapping the EB is the top priority once discovery begins.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible but unvalidated. The Next Step and Next_Step_History__c indicate an RFP will be issued in September or October 2026 for Sourcing, CLM, and Tail Spend. The carve-out from Sanofi (April 2025) is the forcing event driving procurement transformation. Salesforce Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are null, so the approval chain is unmapped. The contact roster suggests a procurement-led evaluation (five procurement roles, no C-level or executive sponsor), but without discovery calls, the process—vendor shortlisting, demo rounds, commercials timeline, InfoSec/legal review—is unknown. The single visible milestone is the RFP issuance in 10 to 14 weeks. Early discovery is required to map the full process, identify gates, and understand who escalates to commercial approval.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unknown. Salesforce Business_Requirements__c is null, and zero discovery or demo calls have occurred to surface the buyer's evaluation framework. The Next Step notes an RFP is expected in September or October 2026 for Sourcing, CLM, and Tail Spend, which implies structured criteria will emerge in the RFP, but Zycus has not yet influenced those criteria. The RFP timeline is 10 to 14 weeks out, and without early discovery to shape requirements, Zycus risks responding to criteria designed around incumbent capabilities (SAP and Coupa per Why_not_Zycus__c). The immediate move is to conduct discovery with Cédric and Lyvio to understand their evaluation priorities and begin positioning Zycus strengths (AI, tail spend via ANA, sourcing automation) before the RFP is finalized.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS score 5.0 (Moderate) signals AI opportunity but low confidence. Product scope includes three AI SKUs (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake), which is a strong AI footprint for an S2C deal. However, AIS_Why__c notes zero Avoma meetings and no engagement evidence to confirm the buyer's AI appetite. The AI positioning is Zycus-driven, not buyer-validated. The score could rise to 7+ with a discovery call capturing buyer AI dialogue, or drop to 3 if the deal remains dormant. The compelling event (post-carve-out transformation) suggests openness to modern solutions, but AI Hungry vs. Curious vs. Resistant is unknown.

confidence_signals.summary: Confidence is low. Deal is early-stage (Qualified, 62 days in stage) with zero discovery calls, no Economic Buyer mapped, and single-threaded engagement. Last buyer touch 2026-06-15 was an auto-reply; Cédric returned 6 days ago but has not re-engaged. RFP expected in 10-14 weeks, but Zycus has not yet influenced criteria. Cooling risk is moderate: 16-day silence post-contact, but engagement state is LIVE per server pulse (rep reached out 2026-06-15, awaiting buyer reply after paternity leave).

north_star_verdict.math: Deal qualified 2026-04-30 (62 days in Qualified stage). Close date 2026-11-28 is 150 days out. Last buyer touch 2026-06-15 (16 days ago) was an auto-reply (paternity leave until 2026-06-25, now 6 days past). RFP expected September/October 2026 means 10 to 14 weeks until formal evaluation starts. At 62 days in Qualified with zero discovery calls and no EB/DM mapped, the deal is behind required pace for a 150-day runway to a November close. Forecast category Pipeline is appropriate given the early stage, but the 16-day silence post-paternity-leave return (Cédric back 2026-06-25, today 2026-07-01) and single-threaded engagement create execution risk. No forward slip recorded yet. Time-in-stage math shows deal has spent 100 percent of its life in Qualified with no progression signal.

north_star_verdict.headline: Pre-discovery qualified deal awaiting buyer re-engagement after paternity leave. RFP expected in 10 to 14 weeks but zero discovery calls completed and no Economic Buyer mapped. Single-threaded through one evaluator with 16-day silence.

flags: Re-engage Cédric Elmaleth immediately when he returns mid-July 2026 (in ~2 weeks) and secure the discovery meeting within 5 business days of his return to extract pain quantification, decision criteria, competitive shortlist, and RFP timeline before the September or October 2026 RFP is finalized.

Multi-thread to Lyvio FRIMAT (VP Global Head of Procurement CoE Strategy and Transformation) in parallel to Cédric, as he is a senior influencer and the Next_Step__c notes Pierre should chase both Lyvio and Cédric; reduce single-point dependency and build a second champion thread.

Map the Economic Buyer and budget authority during the discovery call, as no EB or Executive Sponsor is identified and a 150k EUR S2P deal at a newly independent pharma entity (Opella post-Sanofi carve-out) will require C-level or finance sign-off; the absence of an EB is a gate risk for a Pipeline forecast deal at 155 days to close.
Zero discovery calls completed in 62 days since qualification (2026-04-30 to 2026-07-01); RFP expected in 10-14 weeks, risk of losing early influence.
No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped; five contacts are Evaluator/Influencer only. Gate risk for commercial progression.
Single-threaded through Cédric Elmaleth (Senior Director); senior influencer Lyvio FRIMAT (VP, Procurement COE) not yet engaged.
Buyer silence since 2026-06-15 (16 days). Cédric returned from paternity leave 2026-06-25 (6 days ago) but has not re-engaged.
Inherited SAP/Coupa stack creates incumbent inertia; CLM vendor already selected reduces iContract footprint.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is early-stage (Qualified, 62 days in stage) awaiting first discovery call. Key gaps: zero buyer calls, no Economic Buyer mapped, single-threaded engagement, and RFP influence window closing (10-14 weeks to RFP). Pipeline forecast is appropriate, but execution risk is high without immediate re-engagement post-paternity leave.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus faces inherited-stack inertia rather than active vendor competition. Opella is operating on legacy SAP and Coupa systems from Sanofi. If incumbents or integrated extensions (e.g., SAP Ariba for sourcing) position themselves as the low-risk path, they have an advantage. A CLM vendor has already been selected, reducing the iContract footprint opportunity. Zycus differentiation is AI-driven capabilities (ANA for tail spend, Merlin suite for sourcing automation and decision intelligence) that legacy tools lack, positioned as the modern transformation layer for an independent Opella. This competitive narrative has not yet been tested with the buyer (zero calls).

hard.ais_why:
• Product scope includes ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake alongside iContract and iSource ‚Äî strong AI product footprint in an S2C Pharma deal. Three AI SKUs (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) signal Zycus has positioned the deal with AI as a meaningful part of the value proposition.
• Qualified stage with zero Avoma meetings recorded, no SF Description, BD_Engagement Unengaged, no LastActivityDate. Opella is the consumer healthcare spin-off of Sanofi. The complete absence of any engagement evidence (no Description, no Avoma, no LastActivity) combined with Unengaged BD suggests the deal may be very early-stage or dormant.
• Low confidence: rich AI product scope (3 AI SKUs) but no engagement evidence to confirm AI as the buying driver. Held at 5 ‚Äî would move UP to 7+ with a verified Avoma session capturing buyer AI dialogue; would drop to 3 if the dormancy persists. The 3-AI-SKU positioning by Zycus is the only AI signal.

deltas.subject: champion

No Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor identified. Five contacts are Influencer or Evaluator roles; no C-level or budget holder mapped. EB absence is a gate risk for a 150k EUR deal at a newly independent Opella.

Deal stalled awaiting Cédric Elmaleth return mid-July 2026. RFP expected September or October 2026 (10 to 14 weeks from today 2026-06-26), but zero discovery or buyer calls have occurred. Risk: losing early RFP influence if we do not engage promptly when Cédric returns.

Re-engage Cédric Elmaleth immediately when he returns mid-July 2026 (in ~2 weeks) and secure the discovery meeting within 5 business days of his return to extract pain quantification, decision criteria, competitive shortlist, and RFP timeline before the September or October 2026 RFP is finalized.

Multi-thread to Lyvio FRIMAT (VP Global Head of Procurement CoE Strategy and Transformation) in parallel to Cédric, as he is a senior influencer and the Next_Step__c notes Pierre should chase both Lyvio and Cédric; reduce single-point dependency and build a second champion thread.

Map the Economic Buyer and budget authority during the discovery call, as no EB or Executive Sponsor is identified and a 150k EUR S2P deal at a newly independent pharma entity (Opella post-Sanofi carve-out) will require C-level or finance sign-off; the absence of an EB is a gate risk for a Pipeline forecast deal at 155 days to close.

value.name: SAP and Coupa (inherited incumbent stack)
value.quote: Opella is operating on inherited legacy systems from Sanofi (SAP plus Coupa) which may not align with their new independent structure. Existing ecosystem with SAP and Coupa may favor incumbents or integrated extensions.
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus AI (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) as the post-carve-out transformation lever that SAP and Coupa legacy cannot deliver; emphasize tail spend visibility and agentic sourcing as the new-Opella competitive advantage.
